title: rubus koehleri var. horridiformis
intro: Rubus koehleri var. horridiformis, also known as the Chinese wild strawberry, is a type of plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating headaches and eye problems, and supporting immune function. Rubus koehleri var. horridiformis can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
